# BY-LAWS

**OF THE**

**WISCONSIN ASSOCIATION OF DISABILITY EXAMINERS**

# ARTICLE I

## NAME

The name of this Chapter shall be The Wisconsin Association of Disability Examiners.

**ARTICLE II**

**RELATIONSHIP TO THE NATIONAL ASSOCIATION OF DISABILITY EXAMINERS (NADE)**

The Wisconsin Association of Disability Examiners is a chapter of the National Association of Disability Examiners and is subject to the provisions of NADE’s Constitution and by-laws that pertain to State Chapters.

**ARTICLE III**

**DEFINITIONS**

For the purposes of this constitution:

1. Association means Wisconsin Association of Disability Examiners.
2. Executive board means the Board of Directors of the State Chapter.

**ARTICLE IV**

**PURPOSE**

It shall be the basic purpose of this organization to strive for continuing improvement in the quality of service rendered by DDS staff to all physically and mentally disabled persons. To achieve this goal, we are committed to foster, promote and participate in the following activities:

1. Provide our full support to all public and private agencies engaged in helping the disabled.
2. Provide a forum for the discussion of problems and the communication of ideas between disability examiners and all other concerned persons.
3. Provide a means of communication between those serving the disabled and the community in an effort to encourage better understanding of the disability program.
4. Provide an opportunity for participation in special programs, which will contribute to the knowledge and skills of the disability examiners.
5. Provide an informal setting for social activities to encourage better fellowship among disability examiners and others involved in the disability program.

**ARTICLE V**

**MEMBERSHIP**

The members of the National Association of Disability Examiners who currently reside in or previously resided in Wisconsin shall constitute the membership of the Chapter.

1. There shall be no dues in connection with membership in this Chapter.
2. There shall be no fees, registration or otherwise, unless found necessary and so ordered by the Board.

**ARTICLE VI**

**BOARD OF DIRECTORS**

Composition of the Board:

1. The Executive Board shall consist of the Chapter Officers: The President, President-Elect, Secretary and Treasurer.
2. Powers of the Executive Board:
3. It shall have the power to authorize the expenditure of up to $50 in monies without authorization of the membership when a decision regarding a particular expenditure must be made before a bimonthly membership meeting.
4. It shall have the power to authorize the President to appoint committees to carry out the purposes of the Chapter.
5. It shall have the power to fill vacancies in its own body until the next annual election.
6. It shall have the power to make and amend the rules for its own procedures. It shall keep a record of such rules.
7. It shall have the power to fix the time and place of the bi-monthly meetings and other business meetings as well as interim meetings.
8. It shall take such steps as may be necessary to carry out a program or function determined by a vote of the members and not otherwise provided for.

**ARTICLE VII**

**OFFICERS**

The officers shall have the power and perform the duties customarily belonging to their respective offices, and especially:

1. The President shall:
2. Preside at all meetings, appoint committees, exercise supervision over the affairs of the Chapter and aid in coordinating the work of this Chapter with that of the parent organization.
3. Be empowered to call special meetings of the Board as the need may appear, or shall call such special meetings upon the written request of two Board members.
4. Serve as ex-officio member with voting privileges on all committees.
5. Review the chapter by-laws on an annual basis and suggest revisions as appropriate.
6. Solicit nominations for annual local, regional and national awards. Appoint a selection committee for local awards.
7. The President-Elect shall:
8. Perform the duties of the President in the event of absence or incapacity of the said President.
9. Maintain the membership roles and assist members, as needed, with renewals. Assist with recruitment activities during the year.
10. Collect names of candidates running for chapter executive board positions for the May elections.
11. Assume the position of President after the following year’s elections.
12. The Secretary shall:
13. Be responsible for maintaining the records of the Wisconsin Chapter.
14. Be responsible for taking the minutes of all Executive Board and Membership meetings and distributing copies of these minutes to each member.
15. The Treasurer shall:
16. Be responsible for maintaining the records and safeguarding the funds of WADE.
17. Collect any fees authorized by the Executive Board, have custody of the funds of the Chapter, make such expenditures as may be authorized by the Executive Board and Membership and keep a record of all money received and expended.
18. Submit all records for an annual audit in June.
19. Submit a bi-monthly report to the membership of account balances verbally at bi-monthly membership meeting or in written form to the President or Secretary prior to the bi-monthly membership meeting.

**ARTICLE VIII**

**ELECTIONS**

1.Elections for WADE officers shall take place during the May membership meeting.

2**.** Board positions open for election are President-Elect, Secretary, and Treasurer.

3**.** The current President-Elect will assume the position of President at the time of the May elections.

4. All members have voting privileges, but voting will only take place during the May meeting.

5. Voting will take place via ballot unless other arrangements are agreed upon by the membership.

6. Newly elected officers will assume the responsibilities of their position immediately following the May meeting.

**ARTICLE IX**

**MEETINGS**

There shall be bimonthly meetings of the organization at a time and place to be set by the Board of Directors.

**ARTICLE X**

**COMMITTEES**

There shall be five standing committees.

1. Each committee shall be led by a chairperson.
2. All members shall be appointed for one year and be eligible for re-appointment.
3. Standing Committees shall be Certification, Fund Raising, Hospitality, and Publicity.

1. The Certification Committee shall receive applications for certification and maintain records of members in good standing that are certified.
2. The Fund Raising Committee shall approve and coordinate all fund raising activities for the Chapter.
3. The Hospitality Committee shall coordinate all receptions and entertainment for the Chapter.
4. The Publicity Committee shall coordinate all press releases and advertising for Chapter activities.
5. The Board may in its discretion appoint such other committees, particularly ad hoc, as it deems necessary.

**ARTICLE X**

**PARLIAMENTARY AUTHORITY**

ROBERT’S RULES OF ORDER: The Seventy-Fifth Anniversary Edition, shall be the authority on all questions of parliamentary law and proceedings. This authority shall govern the Chapter in all cases to which the rules therein are applicable and in which said rules are not inconsistent with the by-laws.

**ARTICLE XI**

**AMENDMENT OF BY-LAWS**

The by-laws may be amended by a majority standing vote of those members attending a bi-monthly meeting provided notice of business meeting has been sent to members at least ten days in advance of the meeting, indicating that amendments to the by-laws will be considered as part of the business of that meeting. Posting of the meeting and above information on the bulletin board ten days in advance of said meeting shall constitute due notice.
